description: Top 50 antipatterns across languages and architectures, distilled from 872 reference works

antipatterns.name: God Class
antipatterns.symptom: Class with >500 LOC, >20 methods, >15 dependencies, named Manager/Helper/Util/Controller
antipatterns.cause: Sole responsibility unclear. Repeated additions without refactoring. Failure to extract sub-concerns.
antipatterns.fix: Extract Class. Apply SRP. Use Facade if many clients depend on the god class.
antipatterns.detection: Code metrics: cyclomatic complexity > 50, LCOM > 0.8, fan-out > 20

antipatterns.name: God Object (Singleton Abuse)
antipatterns.symptom: Singleton with global mutable state, accessed from everywhere, untestable
antipatterns.cause: Convenience: 'I can reach it from anywhere.' Lack of dependency injection.
antipatterns.fix: Inject dependencies. Break the singleton into focused services. Test the seam.

antipatterns.name: Spaghetti Code
antipatterns.symptom: Control flow with goto, deep nesting, many flags, no clear structure
antipatterns.cause: Incremental additions without structure. No architectural plan.
antipatterns.fix: Refactor to structured patterns. Extract methods. Introduce state machines for complex control.
antipatterns.detection: Cyclomatic complexity > 20, nesting depth > 4, no functions or methods

antipatterns.name: Copy-Paste Programming
antipatterns.symptom: Same code in 3+ places, slight variations. Bug fix in one place doesn't fix others.
antipatterns.cause: Copy-paste-modify pattern. Lack of abstraction.
antipatterns.fix: Apply Rule of Three. Extract to a function. Use polymorphism for variations.
antipatterns.detection: diff between similar files shows high overlap

antipatterns.name: Premature Optimization
antipatterns.symptom: Complex code with comments like 'this is faster', no benchmarks
antipatterns.cause: Knuth: 'Premature optimization is the root of all evil.' Optimizing before measuring.
antipatterns.fix: Write clear code first. Profile. Optimize the actual hotspot. Document with measurement.
antipatterns.detection: Complex code without benchmark comments; performance claims without numbers

antipatterns.name: Big Ball of Mud
antipatterns.symptom: No discernible architecture. Code organized by file type, not domain. Spaghetti between modules.
antipatterns.cause: No architectural plan. Incremental changes without structure. No boundaries.
antipatterns.fix: Identify natural seams. Extract bounded contexts. Apply Modular Monolith or transition to microservices if needed.
antipatterns.detection: No module names indicating domain; circular dependencies everywhere

antipatterns.name: Speculative Generality (YAGNI variant)
antipatterns.symptom: Abstract classes, hooks, parameters for features no one uses. 'Just in case' code.
antipatterns.cause: YAGNI violation. Fearing future change.
antipatterns.fix: Delete. If needed later, add then. Refactoring is cheap; unused complexity is expensive.
antipatterns.detection: grep for unused parameters, unused config, hook methods called from nowhere

antipatterns.name: Magic Numbers / Strings
antipatterns.symptom: Literal numbers/strings in code: `if (status == 7)`, hardcoded URLs, magic timeouts
antipatterns.cause: Lack of named constants. Quick-and-dirty code.
antipatterns.fix: Extract to named constants. Group in enums or const classes. Document the meaning.
antipatterns.detection: grep for numeric literals in conditionals

antipatterns.name: Dead Code
antipatterns.symptom: Functions never called, parameters never used, code paths never reached
antipatterns.cause: Refactoring leftovers. Features removed but code kept. 'Just in case'.
antipatterns.fix: Delete. Git history remembers. If truly needed, add it back then.
antipatterns.detection: Static analysis (vulture, deadcode), coverage reports, IDE warnings

antipatterns.name: Premature Abstraction
antipatterns.symptom: Generic, configurable system with no concrete users. Parameters everywhere. Base classes with one subclass.
antipatterns.cause: Violating Rule of Three. Fearing duplication. Fearing change.
antipatterns.fix: Delete abstraction. Apply Rule of Three. Wait for second and third use case.
antipatterns.detection: Classes with no callers, parameters with no values, base classes with one impl

antipatterns.name: Bus Factor 1 (Single Point of Knowledge Failure)
antipatterns.symptom: Only one person knows a critical system. They go on vacation → incident.
antipatterns.cause: No documentation, no pair programming, no rotation.
antipatterns.fix: Document. Pair. Rotate. Runbooks. Architecture Decision Records.
antipatterns.detection: git blame: one person owns >80% of recent changes to critical files

antipatterns.name: Bounded Context Bleed
antipatterns.symptom: Domain concepts from one area leak into another. 'Customer' means different things in Orders vs Invoicing.
antipatterns.cause: No clear bounded contexts. Shared database. Direct imports across domains.
antipatterns.fix: Identify contexts. Define explicit contracts between them. Use separate schemas.
antipatterns.detection: Same entity name with different fields in different modules

antipatterns.name: Distributed Monolith
antipatterns.symptom: Microservices that must be deployed together, share a database, have circular dependencies.
antipatterns.cause: Microservices adopted without solving the underlying coupling. Bad service boundaries.
antipatterns.fix: Identify true bounded contexts. Consolidate to a modular monolith. THEN re-extract services.
antipatterns.detection: Cannot deploy one service without the others; CI runs them as one unit

antipatterns.name: Mushroom Management
antipatterns.symptom: Keep developers in the dark, feed them manure. (Pejorative term.)
antipatterns.cause: Information hoarding. Avoiding questions.
antipatterns.fix: Open documentation. Architecture Decision Records. Town halls. Open planning.

antipatterns.name: Analysis Paralysis
antipatterns.symptom: Excessive planning, no shipping. 'Just one more design review'.
antipatterns.cause: Fear of building the wrong thing. Lack of iteration. Over-architecting.
antipatterns.fix: Time-box planning. Set ship dates. Build throwaway prototypes. Spikes.

antipatterns.name: Design by Committee
antipatterns.symptom: Architecture that satisfies everyone by including everything. Frankenstein design.
antipatterns.cause: Too many stakeholders. No decision-maker. Compromise over coherence.
antipatterns.fix: Designated architect. ADR with rationale. Stakeholder review but not consensus design.

antipatterns.name: Feature Flag Hell
antipatterns.symptom: Hundreds of feature flags. Code paths with `if (FLAG_X) { ... }` everywhere. Flags never removed.
antipatterns.cause: Long-lived flags. No flag lifecycle. No ownership.
antipatterns.fix: Flag lifecycle policy. Auto-removal after 30 days. Owner per flag. Quarterly cleanup.
antipatterns.detection: grep for `if.*FLAG`, count feature flags in code

antipatterns.name: Bootstrap Trap (Reinventing the Wheel)
antipatterns.symptom: Custom JSON parser, custom HTTP server, custom ORM, custom everything.
antipatterns.cause: NIH syndrome. 'Our needs are special.' Lack of library awareness.
antipatterns.fix: Evaluate mature libraries first. Use them unless they genuinely don't fit.

antipatterns.name: Invisible Bug / Silent Failure
antipatterns.symptom: Error swallowed: `try { ... } catch (e) {}`. Returns -1 silently. Logs nothing.
antipatterns.cause: Defensive programming gone wrong. Hiding errors 'to keep flow going'.
antipatterns.fix: Fail loud. Log. Bubble up. Return sentinel values only at the boundary.

antipatterns.name: Tight Coupling
antipatterns.symptom: Changing one class requires changes in 10 others. Cannot test in isolation.
antipatterns.cause: Direct dependencies, no abstractions, shared mutable state.
antipatterns.fix: Inject dependencies. Apply DIP. Use interfaces. Pass data, not behavior.
antipatterns.detection: Afferent/efferent coupling metrics, dependency cycles in module graph

antipatterns.name: Hidden Mutable State
antipatterns.symptom: Object's state changes without obvious signal. Race conditions in concurrent code.
antipatterns.cause: No immutability convention. No defensive copies. Setters everywhere.
antipatterns.fix: Default to immutable. Use value objects. Make state changes explicit and atomic.

antipatterns.name: Not Invented Here (NIH) Syndrome
antipatterns.symptom: Custom everything, refusal to use libraries or external code.
antipatterns.cause: Pride, distrust, 'we can do better'.
antipatterns.fix: Default to libraries. Build only what's truly differentiating. RFC process for new libraries.

antipatterns.name: Cargo Cult Programming
antipatterns.symptom: Patterns used without understanding. 'Always use abstract factory.' Singletons everywhere.
antipatterns.cause: Pattern-matching without context. Reading without doing.
antipatterns.fix: Understand the WHY of each pattern. Apply when problem matches, not because 'experts use it'.

antipatterns.name: Death by 1000 Paper Cuts (Continuous Small Annoyances)
antipatterns.symptom: Many small issues that individually don't matter, collectively kill velocity. Slow CI, slow tests, slow onboarding.
antipatterns.cause: No quality bar. No time for fixes. 'Just ship it'.
antipatterns.fix: Boy Scout Rule. Quality time in sprint. Fast feedback. Address small issues immediately.

antipatterns.name: Lava Flow (Dead Code That Won't Go)
antipatterns.symptom: Old code that 'might be needed' but is unused. Refactor 'risky'.
antipatterns.cause: Fear of breaking unknown dependencies. No tests for old code.
antipatterns.fix: Write characterization tests. Delete in branches. Measure usage. Burn it down.

antipatterns.name: Poltergeist Classes
antipatterns.symptom: Short-lived classes that exist only to call other classes. No state. No value.
antipatterns.cause: Misapplication of MVC. Over-decomposition. Procedural code in OO clothing.
antipatterns.fix: Inline the class. Move behavior to the actual owner. Use functions instead.

antipatterns.name: Sequence-dependent Coupling
antipatterns.symptom: Functions must be called in exact order. State shared between them. Setup before call before setup.
antipatterns.cause: Implicit state. No state machine. Hidden parameters.
antipatterns.fix: Make state explicit. Bundle in an object. Use a state machine. Document preconditions.

antipatterns.name: Boolean Blindness
antipatterns.symptom: Functions take/return bool. Caller has to remember what true/false means.
antipatterns.cause: Primitive obsession. No domain types.
antipatterns.fix: Replace bool with enum or named type. `enableLogging(true)` → `LogMode.VERBOSE`.

antipatterns.name: Stringly-Typed Code
antipatterns.symptom: Functions take strings for things that should be types. `'csv'` instead of `Format.CSV`.
antipatterns.cause: Primitive obsession. No enums in the language. Quick-and-dirty.
antipatterns.fix: Use enums, types, or value objects. Validate at the boundary.

antipatterns.name: Singleton Overuse
antipatterns.symptom: Everything is a singleton. Hard to test. Hidden dependencies.
antipatterns.cause: Misunderstanding of the pattern. 'Globally accessible' becomes 'global state'.
antipatterns.fix: Use dependency injection. Apply singleton only when truly single-instance (e.g. hardware).

antipatterns.name: Tramp Data
antipatterns.symptom: Data passed through many layers unchanged. Each layer adds no value.
antipatterns.cause: Layered architecture taken to extreme. No clear responsibilities.
antipatterns.fix: Identify which layer actually needs the data. Skip the others. Use events instead.

antipatterns.name: Anemic Domain Model
antipatterns.symptom: Domain entities with no behavior, just getters/setters. All logic in service classes.
antipatterns.cause: Misunderstanding DDD. Persistence-first thinking. 'Anemic' = data + nothing.
antipatterns.fix: Move behavior to the entities. Rich domain model. Service layer for cross-entity workflows.

antipatterns.name: Call Super (anti-pattern in frameworks)
antipatterns.symptom: Subclass must call super.method() or things break. Implicit dependency.
antipatterns.cause: Framework design where parent does critical work in a hook.
antipatterns.fix: Template Method pattern. Make the contract explicit. Document the super call requirement.

antipatterns.name: Blob (God Object variant)
antipatterns.symptom: One object holds most of the application's state. Other objects are stateless.
antipatterns.cause: Centralization for convenience. Failure to distribute state.
antipatterns.fix: Move state to where it belongs. Use aggregates. Apply DDD bounded contexts.

antipatterns.name: Fire Drill (Reactive Maintenance)
antipatterns.symptom: Always putting out fires. No time for root cause. Same incidents repeat.
antipatterns.cause: No postmortem culture. No time for fix. 'Just get it working'.
antipatterns.fix: Blameless postmortems. Track action items. Time for root cause. 'Five whys'.

antipatterns.name: Lazy Class
antipatterns.symptom: Class that doesn't do enough to justify its existence. One method, no state.
antipatterns.cause: Speculative decomposition. 'We might extend it later'.
antipatterns.fix: Inline. If extension is needed, decompose then.

antipatterns.name: Object Orgasm (Speculative Hierarchy)
antipatterns.symptom: Deep class hierarchy. 'Animal → Mammal → Dog → Poodle' with one instance of each leaf.
antipatterns.cause: OO taken to extreme. Modeling real-world taxonomy instead of behavior.
antipatterns.fix: Flatten. Use composition. Model behavior, not is-a relationships.

antipatterns.name: Refused Bequest
antipatterns.symptom: Subclass inherits methods it doesn't want. Returns null, throws NotImplemented.
antipatterns.cause: Wrong abstraction. Forced inheritance.
antipatterns.fix: Replace inheritance with composition. Restructure hierarchy. Make parent abstract.

antipatterns.name: Solution Without a Problem
antipatterns.symptom: Architecture designed for hypothetical scale/users/requirements. Never used.
antipatterns.cause: Resume-driven development. Big-design-up-front. Fearing future change.
antipatterns.fix: Design for current scale. Refactor when scale demands. 10x cost is 10x delayed.

antipatterns.name: Wrong Abstraction (Leaky)
antipatterns.symptom: Abstraction leaks implementation details. Caller must understand internals.
antipatterns.cause: Abstraction designed from implementer's perspective, not user's.
antipatterns.fix: Redesign. Apply Tell Don't Ask. Move behavior, not just data.

antipatterns.name: Testing Through a Pinhole
antipatterns.symptom: Tests only cover the public API. No edge cases, no internal state, no error paths.
antipatterns.cause: Black-box-only testing. No white-box. Coverage tools not used.
antipatterns.fix: Branch coverage. Mutation testing. Property-based tests. Test pyramid.

antipatterns.name: Flaky Tests (Ignored Tests)
antipatterns.symptom: Tests that pass/fail randomly. Team says 'just re-run it'.
antipatterns.cause: Shared state, time-dependent logic, network calls in tests, test order coupling.
antipatterns.fix: Deterministic tests. No shared state. Mock external systems. Fix or delete flaky tests.

antipatterns.name: Ice Cream Cone (Inverse Test Pyramid)
antipatterns.symptom: Many E2E tests, few unit tests. 80% GUI, 20% unit. Slow, brittle.
antipatterns.cause: Easy to write E2E. Hard to write good unit tests. Skipped refactoring for testability.
antipatterns.fix: Test pyramid: many units, fewer integration, even fewer E2E. Add characterization tests.

antipatterns.name: Test-Induced Design Damage
antipatterns.symptom: Excessive mocking, public methods just to enable testing, leaky abstractions from test seams.
antipatterns.cause: Test-first without test-smart. Mocking at wrong level.
antipatterns.fix: Test through public API. Use real objects when possible. Test what's worth testing.

antipatterns.name: Singleton Facade (Hidden State)
antipatterns.symptom: Static method calls everywhere. Global state. Cannot test in isolation.
antipatterns.cause: Convenience. No DI framework.
antipatterns.fix: DI container. Pass dependencies explicitly. Make state visible at construction.

antipatterns.name: Chainsaw of Doom (Long Method Chains)
antipatterns.symptom: 50+ chained method calls on one line. Errors at the end. Hard to debug.
antipatterns.cause: Fluent API. Builder pattern. Stream pipelines.
antipatterns.fix: Break into named intermediate variables. Use let bindings. Extract sub-pipelines.
